summary.overall_regression: Critical regression: health score 70 → 80 (-10 pts). 4 critical incident(s).
summary.causal_chain_text: Isolated anomalous events (no confirmed causal chain): 'direct path read' (12.8% DB time) | 'log file sync' (7.2% DB time) | 'db file sequential read' (1.3% DB time)
summary.headline: DB Time fell 18% driven by: 14 new application SQL(s) (top: 6379g1aj6gw6n, 1321s).
headline_evidence: Both periods are CPU-bound (bottleneck type unchanged)
Top worsened wait: 'direct path read' 0.6%→12.8% DB Time

comparisons.root_cause_hint: Time on CPU — actual execution work, not a wait. High CPU can indicate inefficient SQL or increased workload.
comparisons.pathology_meaning: Time on CPU — actual execution work, not a wait. High CPU can indicate inefficient SQL or increased workload.

comparisons.root_cause_hint: Parallel query or serial large table scan bypassing buffer cache. Common with parallel_degree_policy=AUTO.
comparisons.pathology_meaning: Parallel query or serial large table scan bypassing buffer cache. Common with parallel_degree_policy=AUTO.
pathology_investigate: large table scans in top SQL

comparisons.root_cause_hint: Foreground session waiting for LGWR to flush redo to disk on COMMIT. >20ms avg = slow storage. High count = too many commits.
comparisons.pathology_meaning: Foreground session waiting for LGWR to flush redo to disk on COMMIT. >20ms avg = slow storage. High count = too many commits.

comparisons.root_cause_hint: Single-block read — index lookup or rowid fetch. High count = many index scans. High avg latency = storage problem.
comparisons.pathology_meaning: Single-block read — index lookup or rowid fetch. High count = many index scans. High avg latency = storage problem.
pathology_investigate: missing indexes causing unselective index scans

comparisons.root_cause_hint: Writing sort/hash results to TEMP. Same root cause as direct path read temp.
comparisons.pathology_meaning: Writing sort/hash results to TEMP. Same root cause as direct path read temp.

comparisons.root_cause_hint: Reading sort/hash results from TEMP tablespace — PGA insufficient, operation spilled to disk.
comparisons.pathology_meaning: Reading sort/hash results from TEMP tablespace — PGA insufficient, operation spilled to disk.

comparisons.root_cause_hint: Session needs a block that another session is currently reading from disk. High concurrency on cold data.

pathology_investigate: frequency of same blocks being fetched from disk

comparisons.root_cause_hint: Multi-block read — full table or index range scan. db_file_multiblock_read_count blocks per I/O.

comparisons.root_cause_hint: Sending large result set to client — fetch is bottlenecked by network or client processing speed. Often secondary to I/O slowness blocking the foreground session.

recommendations.finding: Direct-path full-table-scan pattern detected: 27915 physical read blocks/s but only 1112.9 read IO requests/s (25 blocks/IO — multiblock FTS signature). These reads bypass the buffer cache and will NOT appear in Segments by Physical Reads. Check SQL Ordered by Physical Reads for statements with high disk_reads and 0%% IO in wait events.
recommendations.action: Identify FTS statements in SQL Ordered by Physical Reads. Run EXPLAIN PLAN on suspects — look for TABLE ACCESS FULL on large tables. Gather up-to-date optimizer statistics (DBMS_STATS.GATHER_TABLE_STATS). Verify indexes exist and are visible: SELECT index_name, status, visibility FROM dba_indexes WHERE table_name = '<table>';
recommendations.impact: Direct impact on DB availability or response time

recommendations.finding: Wait event 'direct path read' classified as worsening: 302.2s -> 5687.7s (+1312%) [Both volume and latency increased]
recommendations.action: Parallel query or serial large table scan bypassing buffer cache. Common with parallel_degree_policy=AUTO.
recommendations.oracle_fix: SELECT * FROM V$EVENT_HISTOGRAM WHERE event = 'direct path read';
recommendations.impact: 12.8% of DB time in bad period

recommendations.finding: Wait event 'log file sync' classified as worsening: 876.8s -> 3188.7s (+173%) [Volume increase — stable latency, more waits]
recommendations.action: Foreground session waiting for LGWR to flush redo to disk on COMMIT. >20ms avg = slow storage. High count = too many commits.
recommendations.oracle_fix: SELECT * FROM V$EVENT_HISTOGRAM WHERE event = 'log file sync';
recommendations.impact: 7.2% of DB time in bad period

recommendations.finding: Wait event 'db file sequential read' classified as worsening: 83.2s -> 589.7s (+432%) [Latency increase — storage/contention problem]
recommendations.action: Single-block read — index lookup or rowid fetch. High count = many index scans. High avg latency = storage problem.
recommendations.oracle_fix: SELECT * FROM V$EVENT_HISTOGRAM WHERE event = 'db file sequential read';
recommendations.impact: 1.3% of DB time in bad period

all_metrics.label: DB Time/sec
all_metrics.explanation: Total database work per second. Core workload intensity metric.

all_metrics.label: DB CPU/sec
all_metrics.explanation: CPU consumed by database per second. Increasing means more compute demand.

all_metrics.label: Logical Reads/sec
all_metrics.explanation: Buffer gets per second. High values indicate intensive SQL scanning.

all_metrics.label: Physical Reads/sec
all_metrics.explanation: Disk read blocks per second. High value with low Read IO Requests/s = large multi-block full-table scans (direct-path, invisible to Segments section). High value with proportionally high Read IO Requests/s = many single-block index reads. Compare both metrics together.

all_metrics.label: Physical Writes/sec
all_metrics.explanation: Disk writes per second. Increase may indicate more DML or redo/undo activity.

all_metrics.label: Redo Size/sec
all_metrics.explanation: Bytes of redo generated per second. Higher means more DML workload.

all_metrics.label: Hard Parses/sec
all_metrics.explanation: Hard parses per second. >100/s indicates literal SQL or cursor invalidation.

all_metrics.label: Executes/sec
all_metrics.explanation: SQL executions per second. Application throughput indicator.

all_metrics.label: Transactions/sec
all_metrics.explanation: User commits+rollbacks per second. Primary business throughput KPI.

all_metrics.label: Logons/sec
all_metrics.explanation: New session creations per second. Spike signals connection pool churn.

all_metrics.label: User Calls/sec
all_metrics.explanation: User-originated calls per second. Overall application request rate.

all_metrics.label: Block Changes/sec
all_metrics.explanation: Buffer blocks modified per second. Correlates with redo and DML volume.

all_metrics.label: Buffer Cache Hit %
all_metrics.explanation: Data found in RAM vs disk. <95% = excessive physical I/O.

all_metrics.label: Library Cache Hit %
all_metrics.explanation: SQL look-ups that found the cursor in memory. Low = shared pool too small.

all_metrics.label: Soft Parse %
all_metrics.explanation: SQL executions that reused a cached plan. <95% = hard parse storm.

all_metrics.label: Execute to Parse %
all_metrics.explanation: % executes reusing open cursor. Low = cursor caching not enabled.

all_metrics.label: Latch Hit %
all_metrics.explanation: Internal Oracle lock efficiency. <99% = contention in shared memory.

all_metrics.label: DB Time (min)
all_metrics.explanation: Total database active time. Core load indicator.

all_metrics.label: Avg Active Sessions
all_metrics.explanation: Sessions actively working. >CPU count = saturation.

all_metrics.label: Transactions/sec
all_metrics.explanation: Business throughput KPI (commits+rollbacks per second).

all_metrics.label: Elapsed Window
all_metrics.explanation: Observation window length. Large difference = unequal comparison.

all_metrics.label: direct path read
all_metrics.explanation: Parallel query or serial large table scan bypassing buffer cache. Common with parallel_degree_policy=AUTO.

all_metrics.label: log file sync
all_metrics.explanation: Foreground session waiting for LGWR to flush redo to disk on COMMIT. >20ms avg = slow storage. High count = too many commits.

all_metrics.label: db file sequential read
all_metrics.explanation: Single-block read — index lookup or rowid fetch. High count = many index scans. High avg latency = storage problem.

all_metrics.label: db file parallel read
all_metrics.explanation: Wait event contributing 0.2% of DB time.

all_metrics.label: read by other session
all_metrics.explanation: Session needs a block that another session is currently reading from disk. High concurrency on cold data.

recommendations.finding: SQL_ID azw69yj3vuv2z regressed 105% in elapsed time (5.5s -> 11.3s).
recommendations.action: Analyze execution plan, check for plan flip.  Compare plans with DBMS_XPLAN.DISPLAY_AWR.
recommendations.impact: Directly reduces DB time contribution of regressed SQL

recommendations.finding: PGA memory pressure detected: free memory 17.4GB of 251.7GB (6.9%).
recommendations.action: Increase pga_aggregate_target
recommendations.impact: Reduces temp-space I/O and sort/hash-join spills

recommendations.finding: Execute-to-parse ratio is 74.2% (threshold 75%).  Nearly every execution requires a parse call, wasting CPU on repeated soft parses.
recommendations.action: Use session-cached cursors and hold cursors open in the application connection pool
recommendations.oracle_fix:
ALTER SYSTEM SET session_cached_cursors=100 SCOPE=BOTH;
ALTER SYSTEM SET open_cursors=500 SCOPE=BOTH;
recommendations.impact: Reduces parse overhead and CPU consumption

insights.title: 14 New SQL Statements in Bad Period
insights.summary: 14 SQL IDs appeared only in the bad period, consuming 7401s total. These are either new application code, ad-hoc queries, or batch jobs that weren't running before.
evidence: ftn4uf6ck3bhx: 937.5s, 15 execs — UPDATE /*+ Parallel(16) */ SCPOMGR.SIM_CUSTORDER S
insights.root_cause: New code deployment, ad-hoc analysis queries, or different batch schedule.
insights.action: Identify source: SELECT MODULE, ACTION FROM V$SQLAREA WHERE SQL_ID IN (...). Check if these need indexes or plan optimization. Verify if these should have been running in this window.

insights.title: 1 New Wait Event Bottlenecks
insights.summary: These wait events appeared only in the bad period — they're entirely new contention points.
evidence: read by other session: 196.2s (0.4% DB time)
insights.root_cause: New contention from new SQL, changed concurrency, or infrastructure issues.
insights.action: Match each new wait event to the SQL or session causing it via ASH data.

insights.title: Incident: Critical Sql Disappeared
insights.summary: 4 critical SQL statement(s) present in the GOOD period are completely absent from the BAD period AWR (top: 05kz4x648ukcr, 4m3640xrayvfc, 4r826b5dx6g3w). These accounted for 20098s of elapsed time in the GOOD period. This is strong evidence the batch job or process did NOT complete its primary processing work — it stalled, failed, or was blocked before reaching these execution stages. The lower DB Time in the BAD period reflects less productive work, NOT lower load.
insights.root_cause: 4 critical SQL statement(s) present in the GOOD period are completely absent from the BAD period AWR (top: 05kz4x648ukcr, 4m3640xrayvfc, 4r826b5dx6g3w). These accounted for 20098s of elapsed time in the GOOD period. This is strong evidence the batch job or process did NOT complete its primary processing work — it stalled, failed, or was blocked before reaching these execution stages. The lower DB Time in the BAD period reflects less productive work, NOT lower load.
insights.action: 1. Check application/batch job logs for errors or early exits during the BAD period. 2. Verify the job reached its processing stages (scheduler logs, job history). 3. Identify what WAS running in BAD that prevented these SQLs from executing. 4. Compare the execution plans of SQLs that ran in BAD vs GOOD for divergence signs.

insights.title: Incident: Storage Degradation
insights.summary: I/O wait 'db file sequential read' increased by 432% (83.2s -> 589.7s).
insights.root_cause: I/O wait 'db file sequential read' increased by 432% (83.2s -> 589.7s).
insights.action: Check storage latency via V$IOSTAT_FILE and OS iostat.

insights.title: Incident: Temp Spill Workarea Starvation
insights.summary: Temp I/O waits = 16.9% DB time (was 2.5%). Sorts/hash joins are spilling to disk — PGA workarea too small or query plans suboptimal.
evidence: {'events': ['direct path read', 'direct path write temp', 'direct path read temp'], 'bad_pct_db_time': 16.900000000000002}
insights.root_cause: Temp I/O waits = 16.9% DB time (was 2.5%). Sorts/hash joins are spilling to disk — PGA workarea too small or query plans suboptimal.
insights.action: Check PGA_AGGREGATE_TARGET and workarea auto-tuning. Identify spilling SQL via V$SQL_WORKAREA_HISTOGRAM. Review join order and missing indexes for hash/sort operations.

insights.title: Incident: Long Running Sql Regression
insights.summary: 11 SQL statement(s) running significantly longer than baseline. Worst: SQL 6379g1aj6gw6n — 0s → 1321s (+100%). Likely plan regression, stale stats, or bind variable skew.
insights.root_cause: 11 SQL statement(s) running significantly longer than baseline. Worst: SQL 6379g1aj6gw6n — 0s → 1321s (+100%). Likely plan regression, stale stats, or bind variable skew.
insights.action: Use Real-Time SQL Monitoring for execution-level visibility. Compare plans: SELECT * FROM TABLE(DBMS_XPLAN.DISPLAY_AWR('<sql_id>')); Gather fresh statistics or use SQL Plan Baseline to lock good plan.

insights.title: ⚠ Baseline Already Stressed — Not a Clean Healthy Reference
insights.summary: The baseline period itself was near-saturated (AAS=29.9 vs 32 CPUs = 94% utilization). The problem period degradation is ON TOP OF an already overloaded system. Performance deltas may understate the true severity.
insights.root_cause: The 'good' period was not a clean healthy baseline — it was already at or near capacity. Issues visible in the problem period may have pre-existing roots.
insights.action: Compare against a genuinely idle/healthy period if available. Look at ADDM findings for the baseline period — any findings indicate pre-existing issues. Do not attribute all problem-period issues solely to new workload.

insights.title: Wait Profile Shifted — User I/O increased
insights.summary: The extra DB time is primarily going to User I/O (3% → 19%). I/O increased — data volume change or missing indexes.
insights.root_cause: Wait class 'User I/O' changed by +16.3 percentage points.
insights.action: Cross-reference this with the SQL analysis below to find the specific queries driving this shift.

insights.title: Execution Count Spikes (4 SQLs)
insights.summary: 4 SQL statements have significantly more executions in the bad period. Check if data volume increased or if application is calling these more frequently.
insights.root_cause: Data volume increase, application loop changes, or different batch window timing.
insights.action: Query DBA_HIST_SQLSTAT to see execution counts across snapshots. Check if all spikes happened in the same time window (batch job concentration). Compare table row counts between periods.

insights.title: 4 Wait Events Worsened
insights.summary: These wait events existed before but got significantly worse.
insights.root_cause: Increased contention or resource pressure in the bad period.
insights.action: Check if the worsening correlates with the SQL execution count changes identified above.

insights.title: 1 Efficiency Metrics Degraded
insights.summary: Instance efficiency ratios dropped between periods.
insights.root_cause: Buffer cache misses, parsing overhead, or latch contention increased.
insights.action: Address each degraded metric — increase cache, use bind variables, reduce contention.

insights.title: Workload Change: DB Time decreased 18%
evidence: Good: DB Time 898.9 min, Elapsed 30.0 min, AAS 29.9
Bad: DB Time 740.8 min, Elapsed 40.0 min, AAS 18.5
insights.root_cause: Minimal change — look for specific SQL or wait event shifts.
insights.action: Review the SQL and wait event analysis below to identify root cause.

verdict.root_cause: CPU is the dominant consumer. This means the database is doing useful work (logical I/O, sorting, PL/SQL). To reduce CPU, optimize the SQL with highest buffer gets per execution.
verdict.confidence_label: Very high — clear bottleneck, direct evidence
verdict_factors: Primary symptom: 'DB CPU' at 84.0% DB time (wait class: CPU)
Object under pressure: XPKSIM_SKU () | Guilty SQL: 05kz4x648ukcr

analysis_notes: RULE: ADDM recommendations are guides, not gospel. Validate each recommendation against multiple AWR periods before making system changes. Do NOT increase SGA/PGA based on a single AWR report alone — gather at least 3–5 representative periods.
RULE: AWR averages hide spikes. A 60-minute snapshot can mask a 5-minute crisis. If symptoms are intermittent, split into 15-minute reports or use ASH.
RULE: Buffer Cache Hit Ratio is not a primary health signal. A 99% hit ratio can coexist with a burning system. Investigate wait events and per-execution SQL metrics instead.

investigation_trail.finding: AAS = 29.9 vs 32 CPUs — moderate load, within capacity, OS CPU busy: 80%
investigation_trail.conclusion: Database is busy but within CPU capacity. Proceed to bottleneck analysis.

investigation_trail.finding: Snapshot window is 30 minutes — averages hide spikes.
investigation_trail.conclusion: AWR averages can mask a 5-minute crisis inside a 60-minute window. If symptoms are intermittent, split into 15-minute reports (awrrpt.sql) or use ASH (DBA_HIST_ACTIVE_SESS_HISTORY) to isolate the exact problem period.

investigation_trail.finding: #1 non-idle wait: 'DB CPU' at 84.0% DB time — this is the symptom.
investigation_trail.conclusion: Primary bottleneck category: CPU. Next: find Top SQL by Elapsed that links to this wait, then check Segment Statistics to confirm which object is under pressure.

investigation_trail.finding: Top SQL 05kz4x648ukcr at 30% DB time —
investigation_trail.conclusion: RULE: Always evaluate per-execution cost, not just totals. High total elapsed from many cheap calls = volume problem. High per-exec cost from few calls = broken query. Check execution plans for full-table scans, missing indexes, and bind variable usage.

investigation_trail.finding: Buffer Hit 100.0%, Soft Parse 99.9%, Library Hit 100.0%, Exec to Parse 79.9%, Latch Hit 99.9%
investigation_trail.conclusion: All efficiency metrics within thresholds.

investigation_trail.finding: Hard Parses: 0/s (ratio 0.1%), Phys Reads: 5373/s, Executes: 2466/s, Logons: 12.9/s, Commits: 0/s, Redo: 5.3 MB/s
investigation_trail.conclusion: Load Profile signals detected: Logons/sec=12.9 — connection storm or missing connection pool

investigation_trail.finding: SQL Execute: 52464.8s, PL/SQL: 686.7s, Parse: 24.6s, Hard Parse: 8.5s, Java: 0.0s
investigation_trail.conclusion: Time model distribution looks normal.

investigation_trail.finding: Wait class mix: CPU=84%, Scheduler=9%, User I/O=3%, Commit=2%, Network=0%
investigation_trail.conclusion: Wait class distribution looks normal — no single class dominates.

investigation_trail.finding: Checked 8 tablespaces. Slow tablespaces: 0
investigation_trail.conclusion: No tablespace IO anomalies detected.

investigation_trail.finding: Built 1 evidence chains linking wait events → segments → SQL
investigation_trail.conclusion: Evidence chains complete.

verdict.primary_finding: CPU-Bound But System Is Slow — SQL Tuning Required
verdict.root_cause: DB CPU is 74.2% of DB time and AAS=18.5 vs 32 CPUs. High CPU at the top of Top Events does NOT mean the database is healthy. The SQL consuming that CPU needs tuning (logical reads, full scans, hard parses). Check Top SQL by Buffer Gets and Top SQL by CPU to find the offending statement.
verdict.confidence_label: Very high — clear bottleneck, direct evidence
verdict_factors: Primary symptom: 'DB CPU' at 74.2% DB time (wait class: CPU)
Object under pressure: SIM_FCSTORDER () | Guilty SQL: 965a5yawa5qwh
AWR window 40 min — averages may hide the real spike. Use ASH (DBA_HIST_ACTIVE_SESS_HISTORY) or 15-min sub-reports to isolate the exact problem period.

analysis_notes: RULE: ADDM recommendations are guides, not gospel. Validate each recommendation against multiple AWR periods before making system changes. Do NOT increase SGA/PGA based on a single AWR report alone — gather at least 3–5 representative periods.
RULE: AWR averages hide spikes. A 60-minute snapshot can mask a 5-minute crisis. If symptoms are intermittent, split into 15-minute reports or use ASH.
RULE: Buffer Cache Hit Ratio is not a primary health signal. A 99% hit ratio can coexist with a burning system. Investigate wait events and per-execution SQL metrics instead.

investigation_trail.finding: AAS = 18.5 vs 32 CPUs — moderate load, within capacity, OS CPU busy: 44%
investigation_trail.conclusion: Database is busy but within CPU capacity. Proceed to bottleneck analysis.

investigation_trail.finding: Snapshot window is 40 minutes — averages hide spikes.
investigation_trail.conclusion: AWR averages can mask a 5-minute crisis inside a 60-minute window. If symptoms are intermittent, split into 15-minute reports (awrrpt.sql) or use ASH (DBA_HIST_ACTIVE_SESS_HISTORY) to isolate the exact problem period.

investigation_trail.finding: #1 non-idle wait: 'DB CPU' at 74.2% DB time — this is the symptom.
investigation_trail.conclusion: Primary bottleneck category: CPU. Next: find Top SQL by Elapsed that links to this wait, then check Segment Statistics to confirm which object is under pressure.

investigation_trail.finding: Top SQL 6jpwtcz4k3fd4 at 31% DB time — SQL 6jpwtcz4k3fd4: 197 execs at 70.32s/exec, 486,007 gets/exec.
investigation_trail.conclusion: RULE: Always evaluate per-execution cost, not just totals. High total elapsed from many cheap calls = volume problem. High per-exec cost from few calls = broken query. Check execution plans for full-table scans, missing indexes, and bind variable usage.

investigation_trail.finding: Buffer Hit 99.9%, Soft Parse 99.8%, Library Hit 100.0%, Exec to Parse 74.2%, Latch Hit 99.8%
investigation_trail.conclusion: All efficiency metrics within thresholds.

investigation_trail.finding: Hard Parses: 1/s (ratio 0.2%), Phys Reads: 27915/s, Executes: 2712/s, Logons: 17.2/s, Commits: 0/s, Redo: 5.4 MB/s
investigation_trail.conclusion: Load Profile signals detected: Logons/sec=17.2 — connection storm or missing connection pool

investigation_trail.finding: SQL Execute: 40530.5s, PL/SQL: 968.7s, Parse: 56.2s, Hard Parse: 24.4s, Java: 0.0s
investigation_trail.conclusion: Time model distribution looks normal.

investigation_trail.finding: Wait class mix: CPU=74%, User I/O=19%, Commit=7%, Network=0%
investigation_trail.conclusion: Wait class distribution looks normal — no single class dominates.

sql_stats.sql_text: UPDATE SCPOMGR.SIM_SKU X SET X.MPBATCHNUM=:"SYS_B_0" WHERE (X.SIMULATION_NAME = :"SYS_B_1") AND (X.ITEM , X.LOC) in ( SELECT SCPOMGR.SIM_SKU.ITEM , SCPOMGR.SIM_SKU.LOC FROM SCPOMGR.SIM_SKU, SCPOMGR.SIM_ITEM, SCPOMGR.SIM_LOC RELX_LOC WHERE (SCPOMGR.X.SIMULATION_NAME = :"SYS_B_2") AND (SCPOMGR.SIM_ITEM.SIMULATION_NAME = :"SYS_B_3") AND (RELX_LOC.SIMULATION_NAME = :"SYS_B_4") AND SCPOMGR.SIM_SKU.ITEM=SCPOMGR.SIM_ITEM.ITEM and (RELX_LOC.U_COUNTRY NOT LIKE :"SYS_B_5") and SCPOMGR.SIM_SKU.LOC=RELX_LOC.LOC )
sql_stats.sql_text_full: UPDATE SCPOMGR.SIM_SKU X SET X.MPBATCHNUM=:"SYS_B_0" WHERE (X.SIMULATION_NAME = :"SYS_B_1") AND (X.ITEM , X.LOC) in ( SELECT SCPOMGR.SIM_SKU.ITEM , SCPOMGR.SIM_SKU.LOC FROM SCPOMGR.SIM_SKU, SCPOMGR.SIM_ITEM, SCPOMGR.SIM_LOC RELX_LOC WHERE (SCPOMGR.X.SIMULATION_NAME = :"SYS_B_2") AND (SCPOMGR.SIM_ITEM.SIMULATION_NAME = :"SYS_B_3") AND (RELX_LOC.SIMULATION_NAME = :"SYS_B_4") AND SCPOMGR.SIM_SKU.ITEM=SCPOMGR.SIM_ITEM.ITEM and (RELX_LOC.U_COUNTRY NOT LIKE :"SYS_B_5") and SCPOMGR.SIM_SKU.LOC=RELX_LOC.LOC )

sql_stats.sql_text: SELECT DISTINCT tmptable.user_id, tmptable.priority_id, un.email, un.format FROM ( ( SELECT us.user_id, pr.priority_id FROM ema_user_rule_access ur, ema_exception e, ema_business_rule br, ema_user_subscription us, ema_priority pr WHERE br.business_rule_id = :1 AND ur.business_rule_id = br.business_rule_id AND us.business_rule_id = br.business_rule_id AND e.business_rule_name = br.name AND e.priority_name = pr.name AND e.enterprise_name = br.enterprise_name AND date_diff(:2 , e.time_created) >= ur.delay_minutes AND (date_diff(br.last_notification_time, e.time_created) <= ur.delay_minutes OR br.last_notification_time <= us.time_created ) ) UNION ( SELECT ug.user_id, pr.priority_id FROM ema_user_group ug, ema_group_rule_access gr, ema_exception e, ema_business_rule br, ema_user _subscription us, ema_priority pr WHERE br.business_rule_id = :3 AND gr.business_rule_id = br.business_rule_id AND us.business_rule_id = br.business_rule_id AND us.user_id = ug.user_id AND ug.group_id = gr.group_id AND e.enterprise_name = br.enterprise_name AND e.priority_name = pr.name AND e.business_rule_name = br.name AND date_diff(:4 , e.time_created) >= gr.delay_minutes AND ( date_diff(br.last_notification_time, e.time_created) <= gr.delay_minutes OR br.last_notification_time <= us.time_created ) ) ) tmptable LEFT OUTER JOIN ema_user_notification un ON tmptable.user_id = un.user_id AND tmptable.priority_id = un.priority_id

DECODE(MA.SUM_IO_INTER_BYTES, 0, 1, MA.SUM_IO_INTER_BYTES), 2)) END, CASE WHEN NVL(MA.SUM_IO_ELIG_BYTES, 0) = 0 OR MA.SUM_IO_BYTES = 0 OR MA.SUM_IO_UNC_BYTES = MA.SUM_IO_RET_BYTES THEN NULL ELSE 2))) END) ) END) FROM MONITOR_AGG MA) ELSE NULL END) FROM (SELECT CASE WHEN V1.XPLAN_XML IS NULL OR V1.XPLAN_XML.EXISTSNODE('/error') > 0 THEN NULL ELSE V1.XPLAN_XML END XPLAN_XML FROM (SELECT CASE 2s2u9g82s75rc.fullText: SELECT DISTINCT tmptable.user_id, tmptable.priority_id, un.email, un.format FROM ( ( SELECT us.user_id, pr.priority_id FROM ema_user_rule_access ur, ema_exception e, ema_business_rule br, ema_user_subscription us, ema_priority pr WHERE br.business_rule_id = :1 AND ur.business_rule_id = br.business_rule_id AND us.business_rule_id = br.business_rule_id AND e.business_rule_name = br.name AND e.priority_name = pr.name AND e.enterprise_name = br.enterprise_name AND date_diff(:2 , e.time_created) >= ur.delay_minutes AND (date_diff(br.last_notification_time, e.time_created) <= ur.delay_minutes OR br.last_notification_time <= us.time_created ) ) UNION ( SELECT ug.user_id, pr.priority_id FROM ema_user_group ug, ema_group_rule_access gr, ema_exception e, ema_business_rule br, ema_user _subscription us, ema_priority pr WHERE br.business_rule_id = :3 AND gr.business_rule_id = br.business_rule_id AND us.business_rule_id = br.business_rule_id AND us.user_id = ug.user_id AND ug.group_id = gr.group_id AND e.enterprise_name = br.enterprise_name AND e.priority_name = pr.name AND e.business_rule_name = br.name AND date_diff(:4 , e.time_created) >= gr.delay_minutes AND ( date_diff(br.last_notification_time, e.time_created) <= gr.delay_minutes OR br.last_notification_time <= us.time_created ) ) ) tmptable LEFT OUTER JOIN ema_user_notification un ON tmptable.user_id = un.user_id AND tmptable.priority_id = un.priority_id

fgzb27c5sucj1.fullText: SELECT /* Released_CP.SELECT */ UDM.DUR, UDM.QTY, DFUMDAPNODEMAP.DFUINDEX, DFUMDAPINTRSCTN.DFUINTRSCTN, UDM.STARTDATE FROM UDT_MEASURE_MAIN_2 UDM, DFUMDAPNODEMAP, DFUMDAPINTRSCTN WHERE DFUMDAPNODEMAP.INTRSCTNID = DFUMDAPINTRSCTN.INTRSCTNID AND DFUMDAPINTRSCTN.DMDUNITHIERLVL IS NOT NULL AND DFUMDAPINTRSCTN.DMDGROUPHIERLVL IS NOT NULL AND DFUMDAPINTRSCTN.LOCHIERLVL IS NOT NULL AND UDM.DMDUNIT = DFUMDAPNODEMAP.DMDUNIT AND UDM.DMDGROUP = DFUMDAPNODEMAP.DMDGROUP AND UDM.LOC = DFUMDAPNODEMAP.LOC AND DFUMDAPNODEMAP.JOBID = :1 AND DFUMDAPNODEMAP.SEARCHCONTEXTID = :2 AND DFUMDAPNODEMAP.BATCHNUM = :3 AND UDM.STARTDATE+UDM.DUR/:"SYS_B_0" > :4 AND UDM.STARTDATE < :5 AND UDM.INPUT_ID = :"SYS_B_1"

fr1319w6zr1g8.fullText: INSERT INTO SCPOMGR.SIM_CUSTORDER (CUST, DFULOC, DMDGROUP, DMDUNIT, HEADEREXTREF, ITEM, LOC, MAXLATEDUR, ORDERSEQNUM, ORDERID, ORDERLINEITEM, PRIORITY, QTY, FCSTSW, SHIPDATE, U_DESC, SIMULATION_NAME) SELECT CUST, U_DFULOC, DMDGROUP, DMDUNIT, HEADEREXTREF, ITEM, LOC, MAXLATEDUR, V_ORDERSEQNUM.NEXTVAL, ORDERID, U_ORDERLINEITEM, PRIORITY, QTY, '1', SHIPDATE, U_DESC, P_SCENARIO FROM (SELECT DISTINCT CUST, U_DFULOC, DMDGROUP, DMDUNIT, HEADEREXTREF, ITEM, LOC, MAXLATEDUR, ORDERID, U_ORDERLINEITEM, PRIORITY, QTY, '1', SHIPDATE, U_DESC, :B1 AS P_SCENARIO FROM SCPOMGR.BAT_CUSTORDER C WHERE EXISTS(SELECT 1 FROM SCPOMGR.SIM_SKU S WHERE SIMULATION_NAME=:B1 AND C.LOC=S.LOC AND C.ITEM=S.ITEM) AND EXISTS(SELECT 1 FROM SCPOMGR.SIM_DFUVIEW D WHERE D.SIMULATION_NAME=:B1 AND D.DMDUNIT=C.DMDUNIT AND D.DMDGROUP=C.DMDGROUP AND D .LOC=C.U_DFULOC)) LOG ERRORS INTO SCPOMGR.ERR$_SIM_CUSTORDER('2HR_DATA_ISSUE') REJECT LIMIT UNLIMITED

g0g1yrfw0m46a.fullText: DECLARE v_params pkg_job.t_process_params; BEGIN -- Setup variables v_params(1) := ''; v_params(2) := ''; v_params(3) := ''; v_params(4) := ''; v_params(5) := ''; v_params(6) := ''; v_params(7) := ''; v_params(8) := ''; -- Execute process PKG_ESP_SCPO_2HR_REFRESH.P_ESP_LOAD_SIM_PRE; END;

_sql_text_map.05kz4x648ukcr: UPDATE SCPOMGR.SIM_SKU X SET X.MPBATCHNUM=:"SYS_B_0" WHERE (X.SIMULATION_NAME = :"SYS_B_1") AND (X.ITEM , X.LOC) in ( SELECT SCPOMGR.SIM_SKU.ITEM , SCPOMGR.SIM_SKU.LOC FROM SCPOMGR.SIM_SKU, SCPOMGR.SIM_ITEM, SCPOMGR.SIM_LOC RELX_LOC WHERE (SCPOMGR.X.SIMULATION_NAME = :"SYS_B_2") AND (SCPOMGR.SIM_ITEM.SIMULATION_NAME = :"SYS_B_3") AND (RELX_LOC.SIMULATION_NAME = :"SYS_B_4") AND SCPOMGR.SIM_SKU.ITEM=SCPOMGR.SIM_ITEM.ITEM and (RELX_LOC.U_COUNTRY NOT LIKE :"SYS_B_5") and SCPOMGR.SIM_SKU.LOC=RELX_LOC.LOC )
_sql_text_map.07sryvt95w8dt: SELECT count(RELATION_NAME) from ds_filttest where ds_filttest.FILT = :1 and ds_filttest.FILTSCOPE = :2 and ds_filttest.USERID = :3 and ds_filttest.RELATION_NAME is not NULL
_sql_text_map.0k8522rmdzg4k: select privilege# from sysauth$ where (grantee#=:1 or grantee#=1) and privilege#>0

_sql_text_map.2s2u9g82s75rc: SELECT DISTINCT tmptable.user_id, tmptable.priority_id, un.email, un.format FROM ( ( SELECT us.user_id, pr.priority_id FROM ema_user_rule_access ur, ema_exception e, ema_business_rule br, ema_user_subscription us, ema_priority pr WHERE br.business_rule_id = :1 AND ur.business_rule_id = br.business_rule_id AND us.business_rule_id = br.business_rule_id AND e.business_rule_name = br.name AND e.priority_name = pr.name AND e.enterprise_name = br.enterprise_name AND date_diff(:2 , e.time_created) >= ur.delay_minutes AND (date_diff(br.last_notification_time, e.time_created) <= ur.delay_minutes OR br.last_notification_time <= us.time_created ) ) UNION ( SELECT ug.user_id, pr.priority_id FROM ema_user_group ug, ema_group_rule_access gr, ema_exception e, ema_business_rule br, ema_user _subscription us, ema_priority pr WHERE br.business_rule_id = :3 AND gr.business_rule_id = br.business_rule_id AND us.business_rule_id = br.business_rule_id AND us.user_id = ug.user_id AND ug.group_id = gr.group_id AND e.enterprise_name = br.enterprise_name AND e.priority_name = pr.name AND e.business_rule_name = br.name AND date_diff(:4 , e.time_created) >= gr.delay_minutes AND ( date_diff(br.last_notification_time, e.time_created) <= gr.delay_minutes OR br.last_notification_time <= us.time_created ) ) ) tmptable LEFT OUTER JOIN ema_user_notification un ON tmptable.user_id = un.user_id AND tmptable.priority_id = un.priority_id

_sql_text_map.f0vwyqgf5j65f: SELECT DISTINCT tmptable.user_id, tmptable.priority_id, un.email, un.format FROM ( ( SELECT us.user_id, br.priority_id FROM ema_user_rule_access ur, ema_exception e, ema_business_rule br, ema_user_subscription us WHERE br.business_rule_id = :1 AND ur.business_rule_id = br.business_rule_id AND us.business_rule_id = br.business_rule_id AND e.business_rule_name = br.name AND e.enterprise_name = br.enterprise_name AND date_diff(:2 , e.time_created) >= ur.delay_minutes ) UNION ( SELECT ug.user_id, br.priority_id FROM ema_user_group ug, ema_group_rule_access gr, ema_exception e, ema_business_rule br, ema_user_subscription us WHERE br.business_rule_id = :3 AND gr.business_rule_id = br.business_rule_id AND us.business_rule_id = br.business_rule_id AND us.user_id = ug.user_id AND ug.group _id = gr.group_id AND e.enterprise_name = br.enterprise_name AND e.business_rule_name = br.name AND date_diff(:4 , e.time_created) >= gr.delay_minutes ) ) tmptable LEFT OUTER JOIN ema_user_notification un ON tmptable.user_id = un.user_id AND tmptable.priority_id = un.priority_id

_sql_text_map.fgzb27c5sucj1: SELECT /* Released_CP.SELECT */ UDM.DUR, UDM.QTY, DFUMDAPNODEMAP.DFUINDEX, DFUMDAPINTRSCTN.DFUINTRSCTN, UDM.STARTDATE FROM UDT_MEASURE_MAIN_2 UDM, DFUMDAPNODEMAP, DFUMDAPINTRSCTN WHERE DFUMDAPNODEMAP.INTRSCTNID = DFUMDAPINTRSCTN.INTRSCTNID AND DFUMDAPINTRSCTN.DMDUNITHIERLVL IS NOT NULL AND DFUMDAPINTRSCTN.DMDGROUPHIERLVL IS NOT NULL AND DFUMDAPINTRSCTN.LOCHIERLVL IS NOT NULL AND UDM.DMDUNIT = DFUMDAPNODEMAP.DMDUNIT AND UDM.DMDGROUP = DFUMDAPNODEMAP.DMDGROUP AND UDM.LOC = DFUMDAPNODEMAP.LOC AND DFUMDAPNODEMAP.JOBID = :1 AND DFUMDAPNODEMAP.SEARCHCONTEXTID = :2 AND DFUMDAPNODEMAP.BATCHNUM = :3 AND UDM.STARTDATE+UDM.DUR/:"SYS_B_0" > :4 AND UDM.STARTDATE < :5 AND UDM.INPUT_ID = :"SYS_B_1"
_sql_text_map.fr1319w6zr1g8: INSERT INTO SCPOMGR.SIM_CUSTORDER (CUST, DFULOC, DMDGROUP, DMDUNIT, HEADEREXTREF, ITEM, LOC, MAXLATEDUR, ORDERSEQNUM, ORDERID, ORDERLINEITEM, PRIORITY, QTY, FCSTSW, SHIPDATE, U_DESC, SIMULATION_NAME) SELECT CUST, U_DFULOC, DMDGROUP, DMDUNIT, HEADEREXTREF, ITEM, LOC, MAXLATEDUR, V_ORDERSEQNUM.NEXTVAL, ORDERID, U_ORDERLINEITEM, PRIORITY, QTY, '1', SHIPDATE, U_DESC, P_SCENARIO FROM (SELECT DISTINCT CUST, U_DFULOC, DMDGROUP, DMDUNIT, HEADEREXTREF, ITEM, LOC, MAXLATEDUR, ORDERID, U_ORDERLINEITEM, PRIORITY, QTY, '1', SHIPDATE, U_DESC, :B1 AS P_SCENARIO FROM SCPOMGR.BAT_CUSTORDER C WHERE EXISTS(SELECT 1 FROM SCPOMGR.SIM_SKU S WHERE SIMULATION_NAME=:B1 AND C.LOC=S.LOC AND C.ITEM=S.ITEM) AND EXISTS(SELECT 1 FROM SCPOMGR.SIM_DFUVIEW D WHERE D.SIMULATION_NAME=:B1 AND D.DMDUNIT=C.DMDUNIT AND D.DMDGROUP=C.DMDGROUP AND D .LOC=C.U_DFULOC)) LOG ERRORS INTO SCPOMGR.ERR$_SIM_CUSTORDER('2HR_DATA_ISSUE') REJECT LIMIT UNLIMITED

sql_stats.sql_text: SELECT DISTINCT tmptable.user_id, tmptable.priority_id, un.email, un.format FROM ( ( SELECT us.user_id, pr.priority_id FROM ema_user_rule_access ur, ema_exception e, ema_business_rule br, ema_user_subscription us, ema_priority pr WHERE br.business_rule_id = :1 AND ur.business_rule_id = br.business_rule_id AND us.business_rule_id = br.business_rule_id AND e.business_rule_name = br.name AND e.priority_name = pr.name AND e.enterprise_name = br.enterprise_name AND date_diff(:2 , e.time_created) >= ur.delay_minutes AND (date_diff(br.last_notification_time, e.time_created) <= ur.delay_minutes OR br.last_notification_time <= us.time_created ) ) UNION ( SELECT ug.user_id, pr.priority_id FROM ema_user_group ug, ema_group_rule_access gr, ema_exception e, ema_business_rule br, ema_user _subscription us, ema_priority pr WHERE br.business_rule_id = :3 AND gr.business_rule_id = br.business_rule_id AND us.business_rule_id = br.business_rule_id AND us.user_id = ug.user_id AND ug.group_id = gr.group_id AND e.enterprise_name = br.enterprise_name AND e.priority_name = pr.name AND e.business_rule_name = br.name AND date_diff(:4 , e.time_created) >= gr.delay_minutes AND ( date_diff(br.last_notification_time, e.time_created) <= gr.delay_minutes OR br.last_notification_time <= us.time_created ) ) ) tmptable LEFT OUTER JOIN ema_user_notification un ON tmptable.user_id = un.user_id AND tmptable.priority_id = un.priority_id
sql_stats.sql_text_full: SELECT DISTINCT tmptable.user_id, tmptable.priority_id, un.email, un.format FROM ( ( SELECT us.user_id, pr.priority_id FROM ema_user_rule_access ur, ema_exception e, ema_business_rule br, ema_user_subscription us, ema_priority pr WHERE br.business_rule_id = :1 AND ur.business_rule_id = br.business_rule_id AND us.business_rule_id = br.business_rule_id AND e.business_rule_name = br.name AND e.priority_name = pr.name AND e.enterprise_name = br.enterprise_name AND date_diff(:2 , e.time_created) >= ur.delay_minutes AND (date_diff(br.last_notification_time, e.time_created) <= ur.delay_minutes OR br.last_notification_time <= us.time_created ) ) UNION ( SELECT ug.user_id, pr.priority_id FROM ema_user_group ug, ema_group_rule_access gr, ema_exception e, ema_business_rule br, ema_user _subscription us, ema_priority pr WHERE br.business_rule_id = :3 AND gr.business_rule_id = br.business_rule_id AND us.business_rule_id = br.business_rule_id AND us.user_id = ug.user_id AND ug.group_id = gr.group_id AND e.enterprise_name = br.enterprise_name AND e.priority_name = pr.name AND e.business_rule_name = br.name AND date_diff(:4 , e.time_created) >= gr.delay_minutes AND ( date_diff(br.last_notification_time, e.time_created) <= gr.delay_minutes OR br.last_notification_time <= us.time_created ) ) ) tmptable LEFT OUTER JOIN ema_user_notification un ON tmptable.user_id = un.user_id AND tmptable.priority_id = un.priority_id

sql_stats.sql_text: SELECT DISTINCT tmptable.user_id, tmptable.priority_id, un.email, un.format FROM ( ( SELECT us.user_id, br.priority_id FROM ema_user_rule_access ur, ema_exception e, ema_business_rule br, ema_user_subscription us WHERE br.business_rule_id = :1 AND ur.business_rule_id = br.business_rule_id AND us.business_rule_id = br.business_rule_id AND e.business_rule_name = br.name AND e.enterprise_name = br.enterprise_name AND date_diff(:2 , e.time_created) >= ur.delay_minutes ) UNION ( SELECT ug.user_id, br.priority_id FROM ema_user_group ug, ema_group_rule_access gr, ema_exception e, ema_business_rule br, ema_user_subscription us WHERE br.business_rule_id = :3 AND gr.business_rule_id = br.business_rule_id AND us.business_rule_id = br.business_rule_id AND us.user_id = ug.user_id AND ug.gro up_id = gr.group_id AND e.enterprise_name = br.enterprise_name AND e.business_rule_name = br.name AND date_diff(:4 , e.time_created) >= gr.delay_minutes ) ) tmptable LEFT OUTER JOIN ema_user_notification un ON tmptable.user_id = un.user_id AND tmptable.priority_id = un.priority_id

XMLATTRIBUTES('buffer_gets' AS "name"), MA.SUM_BUFFER_GETS)), DECODE(MA.SUM_READ_REQS, 0, NULL, XMLELEMENT( "stat", XMLATTRIBUT ES('read_reqs' AS "name"), MA.SUM_READ_REQS)), DECODE(MA.SUM_READ_BYTES, 0, NULL, XMLELEMENT( "stat", XMLATTRIBUTES('read_bytes' AS "name"), MA.SUM_READ_BYTES)), DECODE(MA.SUM_WRITE_REQS, 0, NULL, XMLELEMENT( "stat", XMLATTRIBUTES('write_reqs' AS "name"), DECODE(MA.SUM_IO_RET_BYTES, 0, NULL, XMLELEMENT( "stat", XMLATTRIBUTES('ret_bytes' AS "name"), MA.SUM_IO_RET_BYTES)), CASE WHEN MA.SUM_IO_INTER_BYTES IS NULL OR MA.SUM_IO_INTER_BYTES = MA.SUM_IO_BYT ES OR MA.SUM_IO_BYTES = 0 OR NVL(MA.SUM_IO_ELIG_BYTES, 0) = 0 THEN NULL ELSE XMLELEMENT( "stat", XMLATTRIBUTES('cell_offload_efficiency' AS "name"), ROUND(MA.SUM_IO_BYTES / 2))) END) ) END) FROM MONITOR_AGG MA) ELSE NULL END) FROM (SELECT CASE WHEN V1.XPLAN_XML IS NULL OR V1.XPLAN_XML.EXISTSNODE('/error') > 0 THEN NULL ELSE V1.XPLAN_XML END XPLAN_XML FROM (SELECT CASE 2s2u9g82s75rc.fullText: SELECT DISTINCT tmptable.user_id, tmptable.priority_id, un.email, un.format FROM ( ( SELECT us.user_id, pr.priority_id FROM ema_user_rule_access ur, ema_exception e, ema_business_rule br, ema_user_subscription us, ema_priority pr WHERE br.business_rule_id = :1 AND ur.business_rule_id = br.business_rule_id AND us.business_rule_id = br.business_rule_id AND e.business_rule_name = br.name AND e.priority_name = pr.name AND e.enterprise_name = br.enterprise_name AND date_diff(:2 , e.time_created) >= ur.delay_minutes AND (date_diff(br.last_notification_time, e.time_created) <= ur.delay_minutes OR br.last_notification_time <= us.time_created ) ) UNION ( SELECT ug.user_id, pr.priority_id FROM ema_user_group ug, ema_group_rule_access gr, ema_exception e, ema_business_rule br, ema_user _subscription us, ema_priority pr WHERE br.business_rule_id = :3 AND gr.business_rule_id = br.business_rule_id AND us.business_rule_id = br.business_rule_id AND us.user_id = ug.user_id AND ug.group_id = gr.group_id AND e.enterprise_name = br.enterprise_name AND e.priority_name = pr.name AND e.business_rule_name = br.name AND date_diff(:4 , e.time_created) >= gr.delay_minutes AND ( date_diff(br.last_notification_time, e.time_created) <= gr.delay_minutes OR br.last_notification_time <= us.time_created ) ) ) tmptable LEFT OUTER JOIN ema_user_notification un ON tmptable.user_id = un.user_id AND tmptable.priority_id = un.priority_id

d9q2q82g1tuzx.fullText: select decode(upper(failover_method), NULL, 0 , 'BASIC', 1, 'PRECONNECT', 2 , 'PREPARSE', 4 , 0), decode(upper(failover_type), NULL, 1, 'NONE', 1, 'SESSION', 2, 'SELECT', 4, 'TRANSACTION', 8, 'AUTO' , 32, 1 ), failover_retries, failover_delay, flags, nvl(replay_initiation_timeout, 900), decode(upper(session_state_consistency), 'STATIC', 16, 0), failover_restore from sys.service$ where name = :1

f0vwyqgf5j65f.fullText: SELECT DISTINCT tmptable.user_id, tmptable.priority_id, un.email, un.format FROM ( ( SELECT us.user_id, br.priority_id FROM ema_user_rule_access ur, ema_exception e, ema_business_rule br, ema_user_subscription us WHERE br.business_rule_id = :1 AND ur.business_rule_id = br.business_rule_id AND us.business_rule_id = br.business_rule_id AND e.business_rule_name = br.name AND e.enterprise_name = br.enterprise_name AND date_diff(:2 , e.time_created) >= ur.delay_minutes ) UNION ( SELECT ug.user_id, br.priority_id FROM ema_user_group ug, ema_group_rule_access gr, ema_exception e, ema_business_rule br, ema_user_subscription us WHERE br.business_rule_id = :3 AND gr.business_rule_id = br.business_rule_id AND us.business_rule_id = br.business_rule_id AND us.user_id = ug.user_id AND ug.gro up_id = gr.group_id AND e.enterprise_name = br.enterprise_name AND e.business_rule_name = br.name AND date_diff(:4 , e.time_created) >= gr.delay_minutes ) ) tmptable LEFT OUTER JOIN ema_user_notification un ON tmptable.user_id = un.user_id AND tmptable.priority_id = un.priority_id

_sql_text_map.1kx444y0st781: UPDATE /*+ Parallel(16) */ SCPOMGR.SIM_FCSTORDER SET PRIORITY = 30 WHERE NEEDDATE BETWEEN :B2 AND ( :B2 + (SELECT (VAL * 7)-1 FROM SCPOMGR.UDT_GLOBAL_PARAM WHERE COUNTRY = :B3 AND NAME = 'FO_LAYER3DURATION')) AND LOC IN (SELECT LOC FROM SCPOMGR.SIM_LOC WHERE U_COUNTRY = :B3 AND UPPER(SIMULATION_NAME) = UPPER(:B1 )) AND UPPER(SIMULATION_NAME) = UPPER(:B1 )
_sql_text_map.1nws9kw8nyr19: SELECT /*+ LEADING(DFUMDAPNODEMAP) INDEX(DFUMDAPINTRSCTN DFUMDAPINTRSCTN_pk) */ /* GSR_Plan_Cost_UC.SELECT */ UDM.PLAN_COST, DFUMDAPNODEMAP.DFUINDEX, DFUMDAPINTRSCTN.DFUINTRSCTN, UDM.STARTDATE FROM UDT_MEASURE_NSR_N_GSR UDM, DFUMDAPNODEMAP, DFUMDAPINTRSCTN WHERE DFUMDAPNODEMAP.INTRSCTNID = DFUMDAPINTRSCTN.INTRSCTNID AND DFUMDAPINTRSCTN.DMDUNITHIERLVL IS NOT NULL AND DFUMDAPINTRSCTN.DMDGROUPHIERLVL IS NOT NULL AND DFUMDAPINTRSCTN.LOCHIERLVL IS NOT NULL AND UDM.DMDUNIT = DFUMDAPNODEMAP.DMDUNIT AND UDM.DMDGROUP = DFUMDAPNODEMAP.DMDGROUP AND UDM.LOC = DFUMDAPNODEMAP.LOC AND DFUMDAPNODEMAP.JOBID = :1 AND DFUMDAPNODEMAP.SEARCHCONTEXTID = :2 AND DFUMDAPNODEMAP.BATCHNUM = :3 AND UDM.STARTDATE+:"SYS_B_0" > :4 AND UDM.STARTDATE < :5 AND UDM.INPUT_ID = :"SYS_B_1"

_sql_text_map.2qjq3d3u54ajq: SELECT 1 FROM GV$SESSION A WHERE SID = :B3 AND SERIAL# = :B2 AND INST_ID = :B1 AND EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM MANUGISTICS_SCHEMA B WHERE A.USERNAME = B.SCHEMA_NAME AND A.USERNAME <> 'SYS' AND A.USERNAME <> 'SYSTEM' AND A.USERNAME <> 'JDA_SYSTEM' AND A.USERNAME IS NOT NULL)
_sql_text_map.2s2u9g82s75rc: SELECT DISTINCT tmptable.user_id, tmptable.priority_id, un.email, un.format FROM ( ( SELECT us.user_id, pr.priority_id FROM ema_user_rule_access ur, ema_exception e, ema_business_rule br, ema_user_subscription us, ema_priority pr WHERE br.business_rule_id = :1 AND ur.business_rule_id = br.business_rule_id AND us.business_rule_id = br.business_rule_id AND e.business_rule_name = br.name AND e.priority_name = pr.name AND e.enterprise_name = br.enterprise_name AND date_diff(:2 , e.time_created) >= ur.delay_minutes AND (date_diff(br.last_notification_time, e.time_created) <= ur.delay_minutes OR br.last_notification_time <= us.time_created ) ) UNION ( SELECT ug.user_id, pr.priority_id FROM ema_user_group ug, ema_group_rule_access gr, ema_exception e, ema_business_rule br, ema_user _subscription us, ema_priority pr WHERE br.business_rule_id = :3 AND gr.business_rule_id = br.business_rule_id AND us.business_rule_id = br.business_rule_id AND us.user_id = ug.user_id AND ug.group_id = gr.group_id AND e.enterprise_name = br.enterprise_name AND e.priority_name = pr.name AND e.business_rule_name = br.name AND date_diff(:4 , e.time_created) >= gr.delay_minutes AND ( date_diff(br.last_notification_time, e.time_created) <= gr.delay_minutes OR br.last_notification_time <= us.time_created ) ) ) tmptable LEFT OUTER JOIN ema_user_notification un ON tmptable.user_id = un.user_id AND tmptable.priority_id = un.priority_id

_sql_text_map.3z3ty7a66pmvh: SELECT /* DynDepSourcingQueryHint */ DYNDEPSRC.ITEM, DYNDEPSRC.SOURCE, DYNDEPSRC.DEST, DYNDEPSRC.EFF, DYNDEPSRC.DISC, NETWORK.TRANSLEADTIME, NETWORK.TRANSMODE, DYNDEPSRC.SOURCING, DYNDEPSRC.INVTRANSFERSW, DYNDEPSRC.ARRIVCAL, DYNDEPSRC.SHIPCAL, DYNDEPSRC.LOADDUR, DYNDEPSRC.UNLOADDUR, NETWORK.TRANSCAL, DYNDEPSRC.PRIORITY FROM DYNDEPSRC, NETWORK WHERE DYNDEPSRC.DEST = NETWORK.DEST AND DYNDEPSRC.SOURCE = NETWORK.SOURCE AND DYNDEPSRC.TRANSMODE = NETWORK.TRANSMODE AND DYNDEPSRC.INVTRANSFERSW = :"SYS_B_0" AND EXISTS ( SELECT :"SYS_B_1" FROM SKU WHERE SKU.ITEM = DYNDEPSRC.ITEM AND SKU.LOC = DYNDEPSRC.SOURCE AND SKU.MPBATCHNUM = :1 AND SKU.REPLENTYPE IN (:"SYS_B_2", :"SYS_B_3") ) AND EXISTS ( SELECT :"SYS_B_4" FROM SKU WHERE SKU.ITEM = DYNDEPSRC.ITEM AND SKU.LOC = DYNDEPSRC.DEST AND SKU.MPBATCHNUM = :2 AND SKU.REPLENTYPE IN (:"SYS_B_5", :"SYS_B_6") )

_sql_text_map.f0vwyqgf5j65f: SELECT DISTINCT tmptable.user_id, tmptable.priority_id, un.email, un.format FROM ( ( SELECT us.user_id, br.priority_id FROM ema_user_rule_access ur, ema_exception e, ema_business_rule br, ema_user_subscription us WHERE br.business_rule_id = :1 AND ur.business_rule_id = br.business_rule_id AND us.business_rule_id = br.business_rule_id AND e.business_rule_name = br.name AND e.enterprise_name = br.enterprise_name AND date_diff(:2 , e.time_created) >= ur.delay_minutes ) UNION ( SELECT ug.user_id, br.priority_id FROM ema_user_group ug, ema_group_rule_access gr, ema_exception e, ema_business_rule br, ema_user_subscription us WHERE br.business_rule_id = :3 AND gr.business_rule_id = br.business_rule_id AND us.business_rule_id = br.business_rule_id AND us.user_id = ug.user_id AND ug.gro up_id = gr.group_id AND e.enterprise_name = br.enterprise_name AND e.business_rule_name = br.name AND date_diff(:4 , e.time_created) >= gr.delay_minutes ) ) tmptable LEFT OUTER JOIN ema_user_notification un ON tmptable.user_id = un.user_id AND tmptable.priority_id = un.priority_id
_sql_text_map.fg8syxtqhgkuu: DELETE /*+parallel*/ FROM SCPOMGR.SIM_DEPDMDSTATIC WHERE UPPER(SIMULATION_NAME)=UPPER(:B1 ) AND PARENTORDERTYPE = 6
_sql_text_map.fgzb27c5sucj1: SELECT /* Released_CP.SELECT */ UDM.DUR, UDM.QTY, DFUMDAPNODEMAP.DFUINDEX, DFUMDAPINTRSCTN.DFUINTRSCTN, UDM.STARTDATE FROM UDT_MEASURE_MAIN_2 UDM, DFUMDAPNODEMAP, DFUMDAPINTRSCTN WHERE DFUMDAPNODEMAP.INTRSCTNID = DFUMDAPINTRSCTN.INTRSCTNID AND DFUMDAPINTRSCTN.DMDUNITHIERLVL IS NOT NULL AND DFUMDAPINTRSCTN.DMDGROUPHIERLVL IS NOT NULL AND DFUMDAPINTRSCTN.LOCHIERLVL IS NOT NULL AND UDM.DMDUNIT = DFUMDAPNODEMAP.DMDUNIT AND UDM.DMDGROUP = DFUMDAPNODEMAP.DMDGROUP AND UDM.LOC = DFUMDAPNODEMAP.LOC AND DFUMDAPNODEMAP.JOBID = :1 AND DFUMDAPNODEMAP.SEARCHCONTEXTID = :2 AND DFUMDAPNODEMAP.BATCHNUM = :3 AND UDM.STARTDATE+UDM.DUR/:"SYS_B_0" > :4 AND UDM.STARTDATE < :5 AND UDM.INPUT_ID = :"SYS_B_1"
_sql_text_map.fpw9n2mpvrjm4: SELECT /* Official_RE.SELECT */ UDM.DUR, UDM.QTY, DFUMDAPNODEMAP.DFUINDEX, DFUMDAPINTRSCTN.DFUINTRSCTN, UDM.STARTDATE FROM UDT_MEASURE_MAIN_2 UDM, DFUMDAPNODEMAP, DFUMDAPINTRSCTN WHERE DFUMDAPNODEMAP.INTRSCTNID = DFUMDAPINTRSCTN.INTRSCTNID AND DFUMDAPINTRSCTN.DMDUNITHIERLVL IS NOT NULL AND DFUMDAPINTRSCTN.DMDGROUPHIERLVL IS NOT NULL AND DFUMDAPINTRSCTN.LOCHIERLVL IS NOT NULL AND UDM.DMDUNIT = DFUMDAPNODEMAP.DMDUNIT AND UDM.DMDGROUP = DFUMDAPNODEMAP.DMDGROUP AND UDM.LOC = DFUMDAPNODEMAP.LOC AND DFUMDAPNODEMAP.JOBID = :1 AND DFUMDAPNODEMAP.SEARCHCONTEXTID = :2 AND DFUMDAPNODEMAP.BATCHNUM = :3 AND UDM.STARTDATE+UDM.DUR/:"SYS_B_0" > :4 AND UDM.STARTDATE < :5 AND UDM.INPUT_ID = :"SYS_B_1"
_sql_text_map.ftn4uf6ck3bhx: UPDATE /*+ Parallel(16) */ SCPOMGR.SIM_CUSTORDER SET PRIORITY = 40 WHERE LOC IN (SELECT LOC FROM SCPOMGR.SIM_LOC WHERE U_COUNTRY = :B2 AND UPPER(SIMULATION_NAME) = UPPER(:B1 )) AND UPPER(SIMULATION_NAME) = UPPER(:B1 )
_sql_text_map.gb43dxfzhf5yn: UPDATE /*+ Parallel(16) */ SCPOMGR.SIM_CUSTORDER SET PRIORITY = 10 WHERE SHIPDATE BETWEEN :B2 AND ( :B2 + (SELECT (VAL * 7)-1 FROM SCPOMGR.UDT_GLOBAL_PARAM WHERE COUNTRY = :B3 AND NAME = 'CO_LAYER1DURATION')) AND LOC IN (SELECT LOC FROM SCPOMGR.SIM_LOC WHERE U_COUNTRY = :B3 AND UPPER(SIMULATION_NAME) = UPPER(:B1 )) AND UPPER(SIMULATION_NAME) = UPPER(:B1 )
_sql_text_map.gp323wpwmx63s: UPDATE SCPOMGR.PLANORDER SET SCPOMGR.PLANORDER.FF_TRIGGER_CONTROL=:"SYS_B_0" , SCPOMGR.PLANORDER.SCHEDDATE = (SELECT (PLANORDER.SCHEDDATE) from SCPOMGR.PLANORDER WHERE PLANORDER.LOC=:"SYS_B_1" AND PLANORDER.ITEM=:"SYS_B_2" AND PLANORDER.SEQNUM=:"SYS_B_3") where ( SCPOMGR.PLANORDER.LOC=:"SYS_B_4" AND SCPOMGR.PLANORDER.ITEM=:"SYS_B_5" AND SCPOMGR.PLANORDER.SEQNUM=:"SYS_B_6") and exists (select :"SYS_B_7" from SCPOMGR.PLANORDER CSM_FILTER_ALIAS, SCPOMGR.LOC where ( csm_filter_alias.rowid=PLANORDER.rowid) and (((SCPOMGR.LOC.U_COUNTRY = :"SYS_B_8" AND CSM_FILTER_ALIAS.LOC = SCPOMGR.LOC.LOC))))
